metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the 'extracted from digital-service-orchestra' paragraph actually exists in the README branch/state this plan targets, and if already absent, revise the plan to reflect the true current state.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the README currently contains an 'extracted from digital-service-orchestra' paragraph to remove, but no such paragraph exists anywhere in README.md.
findings.location: What section — 'Remove the "extracted from digital-service-orchestra" paragraph'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the plan is targeting the correct branch/commit where 'Runtime' headings still exist; if already renamed, remove or replace this What item with the actual outstanding work.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the README has 'two conflicting Runtime headings' to rename to 'System prerequisites' / 'Python dependencies', but the README already uses exactly those headings (lines 139 and 153).
findings.location: What section — 'rename the two conflicting "Runtime" headings to System prerequisites / Python dependencies'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Read the current Requirements section of README.md and confirm [eval]/[tracing] are not already under 'Development & authoring extras'; update the plan's What to reflect only genuinely outstanding moves.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '[eval]/[tracing]' need to be moved into 'Development & authoring extras', but the README already lists both under that heading (lines 168–185).
findings.location: What section — 'move [eval]/[tracing] into Development & authoring extras'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check/sign/verify-signature actually appear in the README branch this plan targets before treating their removal as outstanding work.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the CLI reference needs 'clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check, sign, verify-signature' dropped, but none of these commands appear in the README's CLI section.
findings.location: What section — 'CLI reference: drop clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check, sign, verify-signature'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cross-check each CLI command listed as 'add' in the plan against the live README CLI table and remove from the What any that are already present.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'add reopen, edit, tag/untag, summary, scratch, session-log(s)' to the CLI reference, but all of these commands are already present in the README CLI table.
findings.location: What section — 'add reopen, edit, tag/untag, summary, scratch, session-log(s)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify which (if any) of the listed deprecated refs actually appear in the README branch this plan targets, and if all are already absent, remove or replace this What item; for SCRATCH_BASE_DIR at line 585, clarify whether the config table entry itself should be removed.
findings.finding: The plan asserts deprecated refs (REBAR_NO_SYNC, --no-sync, REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE, require_verdict_for_close, verdict-hash, config.conf, SCRATCH_BASE_DIR alias) need to be removed from the README, but none of these appear in README.md.
findings.location: What section — 'remove deprecated refs (REBAR_NO_SYNC, --no-sync, REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE, require_verdict_for_close, verdict-hash, config.conf, SCRATCH_BASE_DIR alias)'; AC line 'No deprecated variables/commands remain in README'

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `clarity-check`, `check-ac`, `quality-check`, `sign`, and `verify-signature` need to be removed from the README CLI table (they are currently absent) or from some other README section (e.g., the Python library code block at line 495 which shows `rebar.sign_manifest`/`rebar.verify_signature`).
findings.finding: The plan states 'drop clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check, sign, verify-signature' from the README CLI reference, but the current README CLI table (lines 319–344) does not list any of these five commands — they are already absent. The plan misdescribes the current state, risking wasted implementer effort or confusion about what actually needs doing.
findings.location: What section / CLI reference bullet: 'drop clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check, sign, verify-signature'
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to also remove `rebar.sign_manifest` / `rebar.verify_signature` from the library example block (README lines 494–496), say so explicitly. If the intent is only the CLI table, note these are already absent and remove the bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify docs/manifest-signing.md does not already exist with the target content before treating AC2 as open; if it does, revise the AC to specify what incremental change (if any) is needed.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Move Signing a manifest of verified steps to docs/manifest-signing.md' as outstanding work, but docs/manifest-signing.md already exists with that full content, and README already links to it with a one-line auto-handled mention.
findings.location: What section — 'Move "Signing a manifest of verified steps" to docs/manifest-signing.md with a one-line auto-handled mention'; AC line 'docs/manifest-signing.md created with the moved content'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC 'docs/manifest-signing.md created with the moved content' with a verification step that confirms the existing file matches the intended content and the README link is correct.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or extend an acceptance criterion to explicitly verify that each listed dependency entry in the Requirements section carries a link to its official site (not just that no links are broken).
findings.finding: The described deliverable 'link every dependency to its official site' has no acceptance criterion that verifies the outbound links were actually added; AC1's 'internally consistent (no dangling links/refs)' only verifies absence of broken links, not presence of new ones.
findings.location: AC line 1 vs. 'Requirements' bullet in What section
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 to: 'README edits applied and internally consistent (no dangling links/refs, and every dependency entry links to its official site)' — or add a dedicated AC: 'Every dependency in the Requirements section links to its official site.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a repo-wide search step (e.g., `grep -r 'clarity-check\|check-ac\|quality-check\|sign\|verify-signature\|manifest-of-verified-steps\|REBAR_NO_SYNC\|REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE\|require_verdict_for_close\|verdict-hash\|config\.conf\|SCRATCH_BASE_DIR' docs/ CLAUDE.md`) to find and update stale references outside README before closing the task.
findings.finding: The plan scopes stale-reference cleanup to README only, but other documentation files (e.g., CLAUDE.md, docs/*.md, wiki, or any file that links to the removed README CLI entries or the now-relocated `#signing-a-manifest-of-verified-steps` anchor) are not audited. Dropped commands (clarity-check, sign, verify-signature, etc.) or the moved heading anchor may still be referenced outside README, stranding stale links.
findings.location: ## What — 'drop clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check, sign, verify-signature' and 'Move "Signing a manifest of verified steps" to docs/manifest-signing.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC to: 'No deprecated variables/commands remain in README **or any other docs/*.md / CLAUDE.md**' and add a grep step in the implementation checklist covering all removed identifiers and the old manifest-signing heading anchor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that `docs/manifest-signing.md` already exists; update the plan to specify which README prose around signing (e.g., Configuration section lines 578–581 inline `rebar sign` usage) still needs to be trimmed to complete the move.
findings.finding: docs/manifest-signing.md already exists with the fully-moved content, and README line 386 already contains the one-line auto-handled mention. The plan's deliverable 'docs/manifest-signing.md created with the moved content' is already complete, making AC2 a trivial pass that may mask whether the actual README prose that surrounds signing has been cleaned up.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'docs/manifest-signing.md created with the moved content'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the 'docs/manifest-signing.md created' AC with a narrower AC targeting the specific README sections still containing signing prose (e.g., 'Configuration section no longer contains inline `rebar sign` usage instructions').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise AC2 from 'created' to 'verified and updated' to reflect that docs/manifest-signing.md already exists; add an explicit sub-check that any inline signing prose in README has been removed and the existing doc content is consistent with the README edits.
findings.finding: AC2 states 'docs/manifest-signing.md created' but the file already exists at docs/manifest-signing.md with the correct content and back-links. An implementer who reads this AC will either (a) skip it as already done without checking for content drift, or (b) overwrite it blindly. Neither is safe — the correct action is to verify and update the existing file.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'docs/manifest-signing.md created with the moved content'
findings.suggested_fix: AC2: 'docs/manifest-signing.md verified up-to-date (file already exists); any remaining inline signing prose in README removed and replaced with the one-line auto-handled mention.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for the 'no dangling links/refs' claim in AC1 (e.g., `markdown-link-check README.md` or `grep -n` sweep for all anchor references).
findings.finding: AC1 claims 'no dangling links/refs' but the plan describes no concrete proving command (e.g., a link-checker invocation) to verify link integrity after the README is edited.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'README edits applied and internally consistent (no dangling links/refs)'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'Verified by running `markdown-link-check README.md docs/manifest-signing.md` with zero errors.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief rationale for omitting clarity-check/check-ac/quality-check from the README CLI table (e.g. 'internal/gate commands documented in CLAUDE.md and docs/exit-codes.md rather than the public reference') so the exclusion is intentional and traceable.
findings.finding: The plan drops clarity-check, check-ac, and quality-check from the CLI reference without stating a rationale, but these are live, shipped CLI commands (present in src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py, golden test snapshots, and exit-codes.md). Removing them from the README CLI table without explanation creates a documentation gap for real functionality.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add CLAUDE.md to the edit set for deprecated-ref removal, since `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` appears there (line 139) but not in README.md.
findings.finding: CLAUDE.md line 139 references `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` as a live env var name alongside `REBAR_MCP_READONLY`. The plan targets `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE` as a deprecated ref to remove from README, but CLAUDE.md (an agent-facing guide) is an unenumerated consumer that will be left with a stale deprecated name.
findings.suggested_fix: Update CLAUDE.md line 139 to use `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC` (with or without a parenthetical deprecated-alias note).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the doc split in the 'What' section (e.g. 'standalone file allows plan-review-gate.md and reuse-surface.md to cross-link it directly, and keeps the README focused on the command-surface rather than the trust model').
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for why manifest-signing content belongs in a dedicated docs/manifest-signing.md rather than remaining inline in the README, which G6(4) requires.
findings.location: What section — 'Move "Signing a manifest of verified steps" to docs/manifest-signing.md'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
